passage: The Japanese particle は (wa) is used to mark the topic of a sentence. It shows what the sentence is about.
The particle が (ga) is used to mark the subject of a sentence, especially when introducing new information.
The particle を (wo) indicates the direct object of a verb in Japanese.
The particle に (ni) is used to indicate direction, time, or indirect objects.
The particle で (de) indicates the place where an action occurs or the means by which something is done.
The て-form of verbs is used to connect actions or to make requests in Japanese.
The plain form of a verb is used in casual speech and subordinate clauses.
The polite form of verbs ends with ます and is used in formal situations.
